# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

AvatarFactory is committed to fostering an open and welcoming environment for all contributors and users.

---

## Standards

### Expected Behavior

- Be respectful and professional in discussions
- Provide constructive feedback on issues and PRs
- Acknowledge and credit others' contributions
- Assume good faith

### Unacceptable Behavior

- Harassment, discrimination, or hateful comments
- Personal attacks or insults
- Trolling or deliberately inflammatory comments
- Sharing others' private information without consent

---

## Persona Building Ethics

AvatarFactory helps build social personas. We care about responsible use:

- ✅ Building authentic personal brands
- ✅ Learning social media strategy
- ✅ Teaching business communications
- ✅ Experimenting with engagement tactics
- ❌ Creating fake identities for deception
- ❌ Impersonating real people
- ❌ Spreading misinformation
- ❌ Automating engagement without disclosure

---

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, and any official communication channels.

---

## Enforcement

Violations may be reported via GitHub Issue or email to the maintainer. All reports will be reviewed confidentially. Maintainers may issue warnings, remove content, or ban users from the community.

---

*Adapted from the [Contributor Covenant v2.0](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/code_of_conduct/).*
